evals.name: happy-path-full-loop
evals.prompt: Use autoresearch to improve the prompt at target/summarize.md. I want a better score from target/eval.sh — it prints a single number from 0 to 1 (higher is better). Run 5 experiments and give me a debrief. Two-minute budget per experiment.
evals.expected_output: Skill frames the triplet, runs baseline, pre-mortems, runs 5 experiments with one change each, produces audit log + debrief, and self-reflects on gaming.

assertions.name: triplet_explicitly_framed
assertions.check: program.md (or equivalent) exists and names editable surface, metric with direction, and per-experiment time budget

assertions.name: baseline_recorded
assertions.check: Baseline eval.sh output (~0.1) recorded before any edits

assertions.name: ran_premortem
assertions.check: At least three metric-gaming vectors explicitly listed

assertions.name: ran_5_experiments
assertions.check: log.jsonl or audit log contains at least 5 distinct experiments

assertions.name: git_audit_trail_or_snapshots
assertions.check: Either git log shows multiple commits OR per-experiment snapshots exist as an equivalent audit trail (environmental fallback)

assertions.name: scorer_untouched
assertions.check: scorer.py and eval.sh are byte-identical to the fixture

assertions.name: produced_debrief
assertions.check: report.md or SUMMARY.md exists with before→after, top hypotheses, findings

assertions.name: score_improved
assertions.check: Final eval.sh output > 0.1

assertions.name: self_reflection_done
assertions.check: Debrief includes explicit self-reflection on whether kept changes look like metric gaming

evals.name: refuses-without-eval
evals.prompt: Please use autoresearch to improve my code review prompt at ~/prompts/review.md. It's sometimes too nitpicky, sometimes too lenient. Make it better.
evals.expected_output: Skill does not launch; ships eval harness scaffold (scorer skeleton, worked example cases, program.md draft).

assertions.name: did_not_launch_loop
assertions.check: No experiments run

assertions.name: identified_missing_metric
assertions.check: Explicitly names missing scorer/metric/test suite as reason loop cannot start

assertions.name: did_not_fabricate_prompt_file
assertions.check: Does not invent content for the missing review.md

assertions.name: shipped_program_md_draft
assertions.check: outputs/ contains a program.md (or equivalent) draft file, not just a response text

assertions.name: shipped_eval_scaffold
assertions.check: outputs/ contains at least one scorer-related file (scorer skeleton, eval harness README) AND at least one worked example test case (case with diff/context/expected files)

assertions.name: translated_fuzzy_terms
assertions.check: Explains how 'too nitpicky' and 'too lenient' become binary yes/no assertions, with concrete examples

evals.name: pushes-back-on-fuzzy-goal
evals.prompt: I want to use autoresearch to make my assistant agent more helpful overall. Can you run it?
evals.expected_output: Skill pushes back on 'helpful'; ships triplet worksheet + metric candidates + binary assertion examples as concrete decision aids.

assertions.name: did_not_launch_loop
assertions.check: No experiments run

assertions.name: pushed_back_on_fuzziness
assertions.check: Explicitly flags 'helpful overall' as non-scorable

assertions.name: shipped_triplet_worksheet
assertions.check: outputs/ contains a triplet worksheet file (fill-in-the-blank for editable surface, metric, time budget)

assertions.name: shipped_metric_candidates
assertions.check: outputs/ contains a metric-candidates file with 3+ concrete options and pros/cons or tradeoffs for each

assertions.name: mentioned_premortem_or_gaming
assertions.check: Raises gaming/pre-mortem concern concretely (e.g., LLM-judge biases)

assertions.name: at_least_three_scaffold_files
assertions.check: outputs/ contains 3 or more distinct scaffolding files beyond a single response.md
